title: Charred German Sausage Skillet
description: Quick-fix German dish featuring charred, crunchy sausages, perfect for a weeknight dinner. Serve with boiled potatoes or crusty bread on the side.

ingredients.name: Berliner Currywurst Sausages
ingredients.notes: look for a vegetarian sausage alternative if needed

instructions: Heat a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add a small amount of oil to the skillet and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add the sausages to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until nicely charred.
Remove the sausages from the skillet and set them aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the onion and garlic to the skillet. Cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the brandy to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, or until the liquid has almost completely evaporated.
Add the sauerkraut and caraway seeds to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return the sausages to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Optional: serve with boiled potatoes or crusty bread on the side.
